The architectural style of the single-story brick and siding homes is ubiquitous in US suburban developments.

While the landscape is compatible with Southern Ontario or parts of the Maritimes, the specific style of the brick ranch-style homes and the overall layout are more characteristic of the US South or Midwest than typical Canadian suburban developments.

The combination of manicured Bermuda or Zoysia-style lawns and the specific growth habit of the large deciduous shade trees is highly characteristic of the Southeastern United States. While these species exist elsewhere, this specific suburban landscaping pattern is a hallmark of the US Sunbelt.

The lush, bright green, low-growth turfgrass and the specific foliage density of the trees are more indicative of a humid subtropical climate than the cooler, more northern climates of Canada, where different grass species and tree varieties typically dominate suburban landscapes.

The combination of white plastic/metal mailboxes on posts at the curb, the specific style of red brick residential construction with white trim, and the wide, curb-less asphalt residential roads is highly characteristic of US suburban developments.
